---
title: "Richard's Yellow-eyed Grass (Xyris jupicai)"
slug: "xyris-jupicai"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474975914/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Richard's Yellow-eyed Grass"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Xyridaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Xyridaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474975914/medium.jpg" alt="Richard's Yellow-eyed Grass" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/jsfrancis">
jsfrancis</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264517800">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Xyridaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=170366)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats.

## Natural history notes

A slender wetland plant characterized by its narrow, grass-like leaves and small yellow flower heads borne on upright stalks. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, marshes, and pond margins, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and is most noticeable when in flower, contributing to the diversity of wetland plant communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/170366-Xyris-jupicai)
-   [Local profile](https://fsus.ncbg.unc.edu/show-taxon-detail.php?taxonid=1065)
